Objective: To explore the value of bone marrow biopsy in diagnosing acute and chronic leukemia with reticulin myelofibrosis.
Conclusion: the diagnose of acute leukemia with reticulin myelofibrosis should combine the bone marrow aspiration with trephine biopsy.
Moreover, very few of the patients developed anemia, a serious symptom of myelofibrosis.
The spooky thing about myelofibrosis is that nobody knows why it happens.
